design_philosophy.core_loop: Destroy enemy generators to progress through levels, collect gold coins from defeated enemies to purchase temporary weapon/ability upgrades, then face boss battles. Bi-directional scrolling (left/right) based on player input mirrors Defender mechanics.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (coin collection for upgrades that expire) vs. enemy threat; maintaining optimal loadout within time constraints of temporary powerups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a sentient, transformable spaceship (Opa-Opa) to avenge a fallen father and liberate a whimsical fantasy realm from occupation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to bi-directional scrolling shooter mechanics (Defender-style) within a colorful, cute fantasy aesthetic—deliberately juxtaposing cuteness against mechanical depth and high difficulty. Refuses to hide behind visual charm; demands mastery.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Significant agency through weapon upgrade selection (spread shot, rear shot, laser, lighting ability, speed) and timing of powerup usage before temporary upgrades expire. Player must balance offensive power against survivability and map control.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary weapon/ability upgrades purchased with gold coins dropped by enemies. Upgrades include offensive capabilities (spread shot, laser, rear-firing), movement speed, and area lighting. All upgrades expire regardless of usage, forcing strategic refresh cycles.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through levels locked behind destruction of all enemy generators per level, culminating in boss battles. Each level's generator destruction is mandatory gate.
replayability_hooks: High difficulty encourages pattern memorization and skill mastery
Varied weapon loadout combinations create different playstyle approaches
Bi-directional scrolling allows for spatial problem-solving variation

world_building_techniques: Simple premise: avenge fallen father O-papa against Dark Menon invasion
Level design implicitly reflects Fantasy Zone under occupation/threat
Colorful, fantastical visual aesthetic reinforces whimsical world invaded by darkness

boss_design.philosophy: End-of-level encounters marking completion of zone liberation. Bosses serve as mandatory skill gates demonstrating mastery of temporary upgrade loadout and Opa-Opa's mobility.

faction_indicators: Opa-Opa (player hero, defender of Fantasy Zone)
danger_signals: Enemy generators actively spawning threats across levels
Temporary powerup expiration creates resource scarcity tension
environmental_storytelling: Level design reflects Fantasy Zone under active occupation
Colorful environments contrast with dark invasion narrative
Generator destruction serves as visible liberation progress
